description: A gift needs someone to receive it, and 'wei' identifies that person. The dative also marks a beneficiary, or the being to whom something comes or happens. A destination reached by motion instead takes 'kau' (ALL).
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround into middle-height front 'e'. Keep the next vowel in its own syllable while the tongue rises into high-front unrounded 'i'. Stress 'we'.
usage_notes: The recipient or beneficiary is the object of 'wei' and precedes any direct object of the verb. With 'nuhemi' (grieve), the same relation names the person grieved for.
